---
title: ""
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

::: {.hero-container}
# Autoresearch Macro

::: {.lead}
Can an LLM-guided search discover better macroeconomic forecasting pipelines than classical methods? We test agentic search over covariate selection, data transformations, and fine-tuning for Chronos-2 on Norwegian macro data.
:::
:::

::: {.stat-grid}
::: {.stat-card}
::: {.number}
18
:::
::: {.label}
Variables
:::
:::
::: {.stat-card}
::: {.number}
120
:::
::: {.label}
Forecast origins
:::
:::
::: {.stat-card}
::: {.number}
6
:::
::: {.label}
Methods compared
:::
:::
::: {.stat-card}
::: {.number}
4
:::
::: {.label}
Horizons (1-12m)
:::
:::
:::

## The challenge

Zero-shot Chronos-2 — a pretrained time series foundation model — does not beat ARIMA on Norwegian macroeconomic data under proper pseudo-real-time evaluation. The question is whether automated pipeline search can close this gap.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
metrics = FileAttachment("_data/combined_metrics.json").json()
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-headline
//| fig-cap: "Average RMSE across all target variables (CPI, industrial production, retail sales, unemployment) on the validation era (2006-2015)."

Plot = import("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@observablehq/plot@0.6/+esm")

{
  const methods = metrics.methods.filter(m =>
    ["random_walk", "arima", "chronos2_zs"].includes(m.name)
  );

  const data = methods.flatMap(m =>
    Object.entries(m.summary).map(([h, s]) => ({
      method: m.display_name,
      horizon: +h,
      rmse: s.avg_rmse,
      category: m.category
    }))
  );

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 350,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 45,
    x: {
      label: "Forecast horizon (months ahead)",
      ticks: [1, 3, 6, 12]
    },
    y: {
      label: "Average RMSE",
      grid: true,
      domain: [0.8, 3.2]
    },
    color: {
      domain: ["Random Walk", "ARIMA", "Chronos-2 (zero-shot)"],
      range: ["#95a5a6", "#2ecc71", "#9b59b6"]
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.line(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "rmse", stroke: "method",
        strokeWidth: 2.5, curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.dot(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "rmse", fill: "method",
        r: 5, tip: true
      }),
      Plot.text(data.filter(d => d.horizon === 12), {
        x: "horizon", y: "rmse", text: "method",
        dx: 10, textAnchor: "start", fontSize: 11
      })
    ]
  });
}
```

## How it works

1. **Data pipeline** — 18 Norwegian and global macro series with pseudo-real-time publication lag discipline
2. **Classical baselines** — Random walk, AR, ARIMA, ETS evaluated on rolling 120-origin validation
3. **Foundation model** — Chronos-2 (bolt-small, 20M params) via AutoGluon
4. **Agentic search** — Claude proposes covariate/transform/fine-tuning configs, evaluated and accepted/rejected iteratively

::: {.callout-note}
## Project status
The infrastructure is complete. The first search experiment is pending. See [EXPERIMENT-1.md](../EXPERIMENT-1.md) for how to run it.
:::

## Explore the results

- [**The Data**](data-pipeline.qmd) — Variables, sources, and the pseudo-real-time mechanism
- [**Baselines**](baselines.qmd) — What classical methods achieve
- [**Chronos-2**](foundation-model.qmd) — Zero-shot and fine-tuned foundation model results
- [**Search Loop**](search.qmd) — The LLM-guided search trajectory and discoveries
- [**Full Results**](results.qmd) — Complete comparison tables
